content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of size NNN, construct a simple (i.e, without self-loops and multiedges) **strongly connected directed graph** with NNN nodes where AiA_iAi​ denotes the colour of ithi^{th}ith node.

The graph should satisfy the following properties:


If Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi​=Aj​, then nodes iii and jjj should have the same outdegree.
If Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi​=Aj​, neither of the edges i→ji \to ji→j and j→ij \to ij→i should exist.
If the edge u→vu \to vu→v exists, then the edge v→uv \to uv→u cannot exist.
Under the above conditions, the number of edges should be maximized.
**Note:**


It is guaranteed that AAA contains at least 222 distinct elements.
If AAA contains KKK distinct elements, they will be the integers 0,1,2,…,K−10, 1, 2, \ldots, K-10,1,2,…,K−1.
Find any graph that satisfies the above conditions.

If no such graph exists, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test consists of a single integer NNN, denoting the number of nodes in the graph.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, where AiA_iAi​ denotes the colour of iii-th node.




Output Format
For each test case,



If no valid graph exists, output −1-1−1 on a new line.
Otherwise, first output on a new line MMM: the maximum number of edges in such a graph.
Then, output MMM lines, each containing two space-separated integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge from uuu to vvv.
constraints: 0≤Ai<K0 \leq A_i \lt K0≤Ai​<K, where KKK is the number of distinct elements in AAA.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 100010001000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Nodes 111 and 333 are colored 111, and nodes 222 and 444 are colored 000.
The out degree of nodes [1,3][1,3][1,3] is 111 and out degree of nodes [2,4][2,4][2,4] is also 111, and the graph is strongly connected.
Test case 222: Every node has a distinct color, so those conditions are automatically satisfied. 101010 edges is also the maximum possible number of edges such a graph can have.
